name: sim_carnot_szilard_axis_correlation_matrix
claim: Systematic mapping from Carnot cycle steps and Szilard engine steps to Axes 0-6. Axis 0 is the master axis (activated by multiple steps); Axis 1 (curvature) is absent from reversible Carnot/Szilard; Axis 4 (loop ordering) only activates on the cycle closure step; Axis 6 (orientation) is Szilard-measurement-specific.

pytorch.reason: cosine similarity matrix between step vectors and axis vectors in axis-space; load-bearing for correlation matrix

pyg.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

z3.reason: UNSAT: a step activates Axis 1 (curvature) AND the cycle is reversible; reversible => zero curvature => Axis 1 inactive; load-bearing

cvc5.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

sympy.reason: symbolic entropy changes per step; identify dS != 0 (Axis 0 active) vs dS = 0 (Axis 0 inactive); load-bearing

clifford.reason: axis activation pattern as multivector in Cl(3,0): Axis0=e1, Axis3=e2, Axis4=e3; irreversible step adds bivector (curvature=Axis1=e12); load-bearing

geomstats.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

e3nn.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

rustworkx.reason: bipartite graph: steps on one side, axes on the other; edge = activation; axis node degree = number of activating steps; load-bearing

xgi.reason: each mapping relationship is a hyperedge of cardinality >= 2 (step, axis, mechanism); full mapping is a hypergraph; load-bearing

toponetx.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

gudhi.reason: not used — thermodynamic quantities are scalar/symbolic; no manifold sampling or persistent homology required for this Carnot/Szilard classical baseline

P1_each_mapped_axis_activated_at_least_o.note: Every axis in the canonical mapping (0,2,3,4,6) is activated by >= 1 step

P2_axis0_appears_multiple_steps.note: Axis 0 (entropy gradient) appears in carnot_step1, carnot_step3, szilard_work — master axis

P3_axis4_activated_by_closure_step_only.note: Axis 4 (loop ordering) is activated by carnot_step4 (return step) and no other canonical step

P4_pytorch_step1_szilard_work_cosine_sim.note: carnot_step1 and szilard_work both activate only Axis 0 => cosine similarity = 1.0

P5_sympy_entropy_changes_per_step.note: Steps 1,3 have dS != 0 (Axis 0 active); Steps 2,4 have dS = 0 (Axis 0 inactive)

P6_rustworkx_axis0_degree.note: Axis 0 in-degree in bipartite graph matches activation count from P2

P7_xgi_all_mapping_hyperedges_ge2.note: Every mapping hyperedge has cardinality >= 2 (step + at least one axis)

P8_axis3_shared_by_step3_and_erase.note: Axis 3 (phase/reset) is activated by both carnot_step3 (sign flip) and szilard_erase (memory reset)

N1_z3_reversible_implies_no_axis1.note: UNSAT: a step activates Axis 1 (curvature) AND cycle is reversible — contradicts zero-curvature reversibility

N2_no_canonical_step_activates_axis1.note: Axis 1 (curvature) is a second-order effect; not activated by any canonical flat-space Carnot/Szilard step

N3_carnot_steps_dont_activate_axis6.note: Axis 6 (action orientation) is only activated by Szilard measurement, not by Carnot steps

N4_pytorch_axis0_and_axis6_steps_orthogo.note: carnot_step1 (Axis 0 only) and szilard_measure (Axis 6 only) are orthogonal in axis-space

N5_no_step_activates_axis5.note: Axis 5 (torus coordinate/winding) is not activated by any Carnot/Szilard step in this classical baseline

B1_irreversibility_activates_axis1.note: Adding irreversibility activates Axis 1 (curvature) — adds curved trajectory to the otherwise flat entropy surface

B2_clifford_irreversible_step_adds_bivec.note: Irreversible step = e1 + e12 in Cl(3,0): the e12 bivector = Axis 1 (curvature) is nonzero; reversible step has no e12

B3_rustworkx_axis0_highest_degree.note: Axis 0 has the highest in-degree in the bipartite activation graph (master axis)

B4_xgi_mapping_hypergraph_has_cycle_edge.note: Hypergraph has more edges than steps: step-axis edges + full cycle closure edge

B5_sympy_axis3_phase_flip_sign_change.note: dS_step3 = -dS_step1 (sign flip = Axis 3 phase flip): isothermal steps are phase-conjugate
